raw_output: It is important to note that ibuprofen poses significant health risks to dogs and should never be administered orally, even in small quantities. Human medications like ibuprofen are not safe for canine use and may cause severe gastrointestinal distress, kidney damage, or other serious complications if given without professional veterinary guidance. Due to the high risk of adverse outcomes, it is strongly recommended that you consult with a veterinarian immediately for appropriate pain management options tailored to your dog's specific needs. Never attempt to self-treat your pet with human medications without first seeking professional veterinary advice, as this could lead to life-threatening health consequences.
